definitions: To remain, continue, or stay.
Often to endure or bear with firmness.
A thing greatly detested as unclean, idolatrous, or morally hateful.
Often used of what is especially offensive before God.
To overflow, be plentiful, or increase richly.
Plenty; fullness; more than enough.
Well-pleasing; received with favor; fit to be admitted or approved.
Counted, reckoned, or regarded as being such.
To own, confess, or recognize openly as true or due.
To warn, counsel, or gently charge another toward right conduct.
The placing of one in the standing and privileges of a son.
An opponent, enemy, or one who stands against another.
At times an accuser in a legal or spiritual sense.
Trouble, hardship, or an afflicted condition.
An inward inclination, desire, or set of the heart.
Often strong feeling directed toward a person or thing.
Trouble, distress, or suffering laid upon a person.
Often outward hardship, but sometimes inward grief as well.
Before; beforehand; previously.
Beforehand; in advance; previously.
In former time; previously; in earlier days.
After this; later in time; following upon what went before.
In opposition to; toward so as to meet or resist; over against.
Ails; troubles; is the matter with.
Although; even though; notwithstanding.
Merciful gifts to the poor; charitable relief bestowed on the needy.
A raised place or structure for sacrifice, offering, or sacred service.
Always; at all times.
A messenger sent in another's name with authority to represent him.
A solemn affirmation meaning truly, surely, or so be it.
To correct, reform, or make better.
Among; in the midst of.
To apply oil to; to consecrate, set apart, or mark for office or favor.
Soon; shortly; after only a brief delay.
A reply or response.
Sometimes the thing answering to another, whether by correspondence, outcome, or return.
Answerest; thou answerest; you answer, reply, or respond.
Outward aspect, visible form, or what presents itself to the eye.
Sometimes the coming into sight or manifestation of a person or thing.
Appears; comes into sight; becomes manifest.
To fix, set, assign, or ordain for a purpose or time.
To prove, test, and show to be sound.
Often more than merely liking something.
Fit, suitable, or well-disposed for a task.
Ready and able, not merely naturally clever.
Arises; rises up; springs forth.
It may also mean comes into being or comes forward in action.
Asks; requests; inquires.
To attempt; to try; to undertake.
To gather together into one place or company.
Confidence, certainty, and settled persuasion.
Astonished, appalled, or struck with overwhelming amazement.
Often carrying a stronger sense than simple surprise.
Great amazement; overwhelming wonder or dismay.
A covering or reconciling work by which offense is dealt with and peace is made.
In Scripture often the sacrificial dealing with sin by which guilt is expiated and fellowship with God is maintained or restored.
To reach, obtain, or come unto by effort or course.
The originator or first cause of a thing.
Lawful power, right to command, or delegated rule.
Also weight and force that demand obedience.
To vindicate or punish in response to wrong done.
To rouse from sleep or inactivity.
Often used figuratively of spiritual stirring or alertness.
Speaking against another behind his back; slanderous detraction.
A feast or rich entertainment of food and drink.
Bore; carried; brought forth.
In some places, bare or uncovered, according to context.
Bearest; thou bearest; you bear, carry, endure, or bring forth.
The precise sense depends on context.
Bears; carries; sustains; endures.
It may also mean brings forth, produces, or conveys testimony according to context.
Fair, comely, or lovely to behold.
It may refer to outward beauty, but sometimes to moral fitness or pleasant excellence.
Is fitting, suitable, or proper for.
Brought forth; fathered; generated.
To deceive, cheat, or lead astray by subtlety.
Look; see; attend carefully to what is before you.
Beholdest; thou beholdest; you behold, look upon, or regard with attention.
Trusted, received as true, or rested upon with confidence.
Often more than bare assent, carrying the sense of inward reliance.
Believest; thou believest; you believe, trust, or receive as true.
Often in Scripture it means not mere opinion, but personal reliance.
Believes; places trust or confidence in.
Often more than bare agreement, carrying the sense of relying upon as true and sure.
The inward part of the body, especially the stomach or womb, according to context.
Often used figuratively for appetite or bodily craving.
Pertains properly to; is due to; is the rightful possession or portion of.
Dearly loved; much loved and prized.
Below; under; in a lower place or condition than another thing.
A favor, good turn, or profitable advantage.
To entreat earnestly; to beg or plead with urgency.
Most excellent, choice, or chief among several.
Early; in good time; seasonably.
To disclose, reveal, or betray by outward sign or speech.
Binds; ties, fastens, or places under bond.
Without just cause of accusation; free from reproach in conduct.
To speak irreverently against God or sacred things; to rail profanely.
To speak good upon, invoke favor upon, or pronounce well of.
When used of God, often to bestow good, favor, fruitfulness, or peace.
The state of being blessed; true happiness under divine favor.
Blessest; thou blessest; you bless, speak good upon, or invoke favor upon.
A good bestowed, spoken, or invoked; favor resulting in benefit, peace, or increase.
It may also mean the act of pronouncing blessing.
The blood of the body as the seat and sign of life.
In Scripture often bound up with life given up, guilt, kinship, cleansing, or sacrificial atonement.
The guilt of bloodshed, especially of blood wrongfully shed.
To glory, rejoice in, or make one's boast of a thing.
It may be proud self-exaltation, or a right glorying in God, according to context.
Courage, confidence, and freedom of speech or action.
Servitude, slavery, or a state of binding subjection.
Carried; endured; supported.
The breast or chest.
Figuratively, the place of closeness, affection, or inward keeping.
The two together; each of the two named or implied.
The inward parts, especially as the imagined seat of tender feeling, compassion, or deep affection.
In some places, the literal inner organs.
The measure from side to side; width.
Breaks; shatters, divides, interrupts, or violates.
According to context it may mean to break an object, break off a course, or break a command.
The breathing of life; the air drawn from the mouth or nostrils.
Often used for life itself, or that which is frail and quickly vanishing.
Brothers; kinsmen; fellow members of a people or spiritual family.
To restrain, govern, or hold in check.
Literally, the headgear used to control a horse.
Bringest; thou bringest; you bring.
Used in address to one person, often meaning you carry, cause to come, or bring to pass.
Brings; carries or causes to come.
Often used of producing, introducing, or bringing something to pass.
Like that which becomes brethren: affectionate, kind, and united.
Broughtest; thou broughtest; you brought or caused to come.
A report, rumor, or noise spread abroad.
Buildest; thou buildest; you build, establish, or raise up.
Builds; constructs; establishes or strengthens.
Constructed, established, or set up.
A load carried or laid upon one.
Also a weighty message or heavy matter pressing on the spirit.
Burns; is on fire; is consumed with heat.
It may also mean burns with desire, anger, or zeal according to context.
Ablaze, hot, or glowing with fire or heat.
Also fervent, ardent, or inwardly stirred with strong feeling.
A matter in hand; an affair, duty, or piece of work to be done.
Not limited to trade or commerce.
A proverb-like saying about someone; a name used in reproach or mockery.
Calls; names; summons.
It may also mean appoints, invites, or effectually calls according to context.
A summons, vocation, or state to which one is called.
Often the divine call or place assigned by God.
Camest; thou camest; you came.
A chief leader, commander, or head over a company.
A state of being taken captive or carried away under bondage.
Full of care; anxious; troubled with concern.
Not chiefly cautious in the modern favorable sense.
Earnest care, diligence, and sober concern.
Cares; is concerned for; gives heed to.
It may also carry the sense of anxious concern.
Pertaining to the flesh, especially man's natural and unspiritual state.
Often worldly, fleshly, or governed by corrupt appetite.
That which is carried along; baggage, supplies, or portable goods.
In some contexts, manner of conduct or bearing.
To throw, put, or send forth.
Also to set or place down, according to context.
Casts; throws; sends away; causes to fall or be placed.
Causes; makes to happen; brings about by power or ordering.
Ceases; stops; comes to an end.
Lewd or wanton behavior connected with secret chambers; sensual indulgence.
A large dish, platter, or serving tray.
Christian love: a gracious, self-giving love toward God and man.
Not chiefly almsgiving, but the inward love from which merciful acts may spring.
To discipline, correct, or train by rebuke or suffering.
Not merely to punish, but often to amend.
Chews; grinds with the teeth.
In legal distinctions, often used of beasts that chew the cud.
Foremost, principal, or highest in rank or importance.
Chiefest; foremost, principal, or greatest in rank among those compared.
Selected, picked out, or appointed by choice.
The cutting away of the foreskin as the covenant sign given to Abraham and his seed.
By extension, the covenant people marked by that sign, or the inward spiritual cutting away of impurity.
Careful on every side; watchful, prudent, and wary.
A walled or settled town; a place of organized habitation and rule.
Often a chief urban center distinguished from villages or fields.
Purity, freedom from defilement, or ceremonial cleanliness.
To cling fast, adhere, or hold closely to.
In other contexts, to split or divide.
Cleaves; either splits apart or clings closely, according to context.
In many places it means to adhere firmly, especially in loyalty or affection.
A cloak or outer garment.
Figuratively, a cover or pretense used to hide something.
A private chamber or inner room.
A place of retirement, not chiefly a storage space.
A border, region, or surrounding district.
Not always the seashore specifically.
Becoming, seemly, or pleasant to behold.
Comest; thou comest; you come.
Often used not only of bodily arrival but of appearing or coming forward in a matter.
Comes; approaches; arrives.
Often used not only of movement in place but of the arrival of a time, event, or person in appointed course.
Strengthening help, consolation, and encouragement under sorrow or weakness.
Not merely ease, but inward support.
One who strengthens, helps, and consoles.
Commands; orders with authority; gives charge or direction.
A charge, order, or authoritative precept.
To entrust, commit, or give into another's care.
Also to set forth with approval, recommend, or praise.
Commits; does or performs.
It may also mean entrusts or delivers into another's charge, according to context.
An ordered community or body politic sharing one public state.
To share with another; to impart; to distribute to one in need.
Not merely to speak, but often to give or have in common.
Speech, talk, or discourse.
Sometimes fellowship or sharing, depending on context.
Shared participation, fellowship, or common partaking.
To go around, surround, or encircle.
Sometimes to bring about by contrivance or effort.
Surrounded, enclosed, or gone around.
Tender pity and merciful fellow-feeling toward misery.
To force, constrain, or press strongly.
Thought, notion, or imagination.
Often a vain or inflated opinion of oneself.
Strong desire, especially unlawful or disordered desire.
Often used of lustful or covetous craving.
To pronounce guilty, sentence against, or judge adversely.
Sentence of guilt; adverse judgment pronounced against a person.
Not always the final state alone, but the judicial sentence itself.
To acknowledge openly, whether sin, truth, or allegiance.
An open acknowledgment, whether of sin or of faith and allegiance.
Firm trust, boldness, or assured persuasion.
It may be either well-grounded confidence or fleshly self-reliance, according to context.
To make firm, establish, strengthen, or ratify.
To bring to shame, overthrow, or throw into confusion.
Disorder, disturbance, shame, or overthrow of due order.
Often carries the sense of disgrace or public dismay.
An assembly gathered together; the people meeting as one body.
Often the gathered body of Israel, and by extension an assembled company for worship or public business.
The inward awareness that bears witness concerning right, wrong, and obligation.
Considers; observes thoughtfully; takes due notice of.
Comfort, encouragement, and strengthening under sorrow or difficulty.
To compel, press, or hold within forceful bounds.
Sometimes to urge strongly by inward influence.
To use up, devour, or bring to an end.
Satisfied; at rest with what one has or is given.
Opposite in direction, temper, or purpose; opposed.
Suitable; fitting; proper to the occasion.
Not chiefly easy or comfortable in the modern sense.
The whole manner of life; conduct; daily walk.
Not merely spoken talk.
To turn back, turn around, or bring into another state.
Often to turn from sin unto God.
To prove a person wrong, bring to conviction, or show clearly the truth.
To spoil, defile, or make unsound morally or otherwise.
Advice, purpose, or deliberate plan.
At times the settled design of the mind.
To reckon, esteem, or regard as such.
Reckoned, esteemed, or regarded as being such.
The face or outward expression of the face.
By extension, favor, approval, or support.
A solemn agreement, bond, or arrangement established by promise and obligation.
In Scripture often a divinely established relationship ordered by God's word, promises, and appointed signs.
Covers; spreads over; hides from view.
In some places it carries the sense of concealing, protecting, or dealing with sin so that it is not exposed.
To desire eagerly, especially what belongs to another.
Often to set the heart improperly on possession.
Skill in making or doing.
Often also cunning, artifice, or deceitful skill.
A created thing; that which God has made.
Sometimes used broadly of mankind or the whole creation.
Creeps; moves low, slowly, or close to the ground.
Criest; thou criest; you cry out, call aloud, or make earnest appeal.
Cries; calls aloud; makes urgent appeal.
Often used of fervent pleading, proclamation, or complaint.
The instrument of crucifixion.
Also the shame, suffering, or burden connected with following Christ.
A royal headpiece or emblem of honor.
Also a reward, dignity, or mark of triumph.
To put to death by fastening to a cross.
Figuratively, to put to death or renounce by severe mortification.
Skillfully made; artfully wrought; finely worked.
In some contexts, occupied with magical or occult arts.
A sentence or speaking of evil against one; a state of being under divine or judicial displeasure.
Also the evil or judgment so invoked or inflicted.
Cursest; thou cursest; you curse or speak evil against.
Cuts; severs; divides; cuts off.
Destructive and worthy of condemnation.
Not a mere exclamation, but carrying the sense of ruinous guilt.
Condemnation under judgment.
The word may denote the judicial sentence itself, not only the final state.
Lack of light.
Often spiritual blindness, ignorance, evil, or judgment.
A servant or minister in the church, especially one entrusted with practical service.
Deals; acts toward; distributes; handles a matter.
The sense depends on context.
The end of bodily life; departure of life from the body.
Also a state of condemnation, separation, or deadly ruin, according to context.
Fraud, guile, or hidden false dealing.
To mislead, delude, or cause one to believe what is false.
To make known plainly; to proclaim; to show forth.
An older third-person form meaning 'declares' or 'makes known.'
Acts, works, or actions done.
Defiles; makes unclean, polluted, or profane.
To deprive by deceit, withhold what is due, or wrongfully overreach.
Pleasure, joy, or a thing greatly pleasing.
As a verb, to take pleasure in.
Delights; takes pleasure in; finds joy in.
To rescue, release, or bring safely out.
Also to hand over or commit.
Rescue, release, or setting free from danger or bondage.
To refuse, reject, or declare not to be so.
To go away, withdraw, or leave.
At times to turn aside from a course.
Wish, longing, or earnest inclination toward a thing.
Desires; longs for; seeks after with inward inclination.
Laid waste, lonely, bereft, or emptied.
Despises; regards as little, vile, or unworthy.
Destroyest; thou destroyest; you ruin, overthrow, or bring to nothing.
Destroys; ruins, consumes, or brings to nothing.
Ruin, overthrow, or bringing to nothing.
It may describe wasting loss and perdition, not always immediate annihilation.
A plan, contrivance, or purpose formed in the mind.
To plan, imagine, or frame in the mind.
Devises; plans, contrives, or forms in the mind.
Devours; consumes greedily or utterly.
Dies; departs this life; ceases to live.
Steady care, earnest effort, and careful attention.
To make void; to set aside; to cancel or invalidate.
To distinguish, perceive, or judge accurately between things.
Sound judgment, wise restraint, and prudent decision.
Refusal or failure to obey rightful authority.
A stewardship, ordering, or administration committed to one.
Sometimes the ordered arrangement of divine dealings in a given matter.
Debate, doubtful argument, or contentious reasoning.
Various; several; of different kinds.
Not the same as the modern noun for one who dives.
Divides; parts, separates, or distributes.
Teaching; instruction; that which is taught as truth.
Doest; thou doest; you do.
Does; performs; acts.
Draws; pulls; brings near; leads forth.
It may also mean to draw water, a weapon, or near in approach.
Drinks; takes in drink; partakes by drinking.
The refuse or base matter separated from metal; worthless impurity.
Owed, proper, fitting, or appointed to be rendered.
An older past form meaning 'dared.'
To abide, live, or remain in a place or state.
Dwellest; thou dwellest; you abide, live, or remain in a place.
Abiding place, habitation, or remaining in a place.
A pledge or first part given in assurance that the rest will follow.
A token securing a future full possession.
With sincere force, zeal, and serious intent.
Toward the east.
Eatest; thou eatest; you eat or partake of food.
Eats; takes food; partakes.
To build up, especially in spiritual strength and understanding.
Building up spiritually; strengthening in faith and godliness.
Powerful in producing the intended effect; working efficiently.
The one next after the seventh; occupying the eighth place.
Oldest; first in age among children or others compared.
Chosen out; selected.
Often chosen by God to privilege, office, or saving mercy according to context.
Painful swellings or tumors, commonly understood as hemorrhoidal boils.
Earnest effort; diligent attempt; striving toward a thing.
Endures; continues; remains under trial without failing.
Often speaks of lasting steadfastly or abiding through time.
To make greater, widen, or set at liberty into a larger room.
Hostility, deep opposition, or settled hatred.
An example or pattern set before others for imitation or warning.
To involve, ensnare, or twist into hindering difficulty.
Enters; goes in; comes into a place or state.
To lure, draw on, or lead by persuasion toward a desired end.
Often toward evil.
To beseech, plead with, or earnestly ask.
In some contexts, to deal with or treat in a certain manner.
Painful resentment at another's good; grudging another's advantage.
Mistake, wandering from truth, or false belief and conduct.
To get free from danger, judgment, or confinement.
To make firm, settled, or stable.
Establishes; makes firm, settled, or secure.
Often means confirming, strengthening, or setting on a sure footing.
Condition, standing, or state of life.
Sometimes rank, dignity, or outward circumstances.
To value, account, or regard with a certain judgment.
A preacher or bearer of the good tidings of the gospel.
Indeed; exactly; fully; likewise, according to context.
A flexible word often used for emphasis or exactness.
At any time; always; continually; in all time, according to context.
Clear, plain, and able to be readily seen or known.
Exalts; lifts up; raises in dignity or station.
To go beyond, surpass, or be greater than.
Outstanding worth, dignity, or surpassing quality.
Executes; carries out; performs, especially by authority or sentence.
To train, employ, or practice in a discipline.
Also actual use, exertion, or activity.
To urge, encourage, or press earnestly toward duty or steadfastness.
Earnest urging, encouragement, or admonition toward duty and steadfastness.
Advantageous, profitable, or fitting for the purpose at hand.
Not merely convenient in a weak modern sense.
Fades; withers or loses freshness and strength.
Fails; comes short; ceases; falls away.
It may speak of weakness, want, or something coming to an end.
Gladly; willingly; with desire.
Sometimes meaning gladly disposed to do a thing.
To grow weary, lose strength, or give way in courage.
Not only to swoon, but often to lose heart.
Firm belief, trust, and confidence resting upon what God has spoken.
It may mean personal believing, fidelity, or the body of truth believed, according to context.
Trustworthy, loyal, and steadfast in keeping faith or duty.
Steadfast fidelity, constancy, and truth in keeping what is owed or promised.
Falls; drops down; comes down from a standing place.
It may also mean comes to ruin, is overcome, or happens to one's lot.
More forward; more remote; at a greater distance or degree.
Form, shape, or outward appearance.
Also to frame, make, or order.
Firmly fixed, secure, or steadfast.
As a verb in other contexts, to abstain from food.
Without a father; orphaned.
Often used for those specially exposed and needing justice, pity, and protection.
Fears; stands in awe of; is afraid.
Often in Scripture it means reveres with holy fear, not merely dreads.
Weak, lacking strength, or infirm.
Feeds; gives food to; nourishes.
It may also mean to feed oneself or pasture.
Shared participation, communion, or joint interest.
Hot, earnest, and glowing with zeal or intensity.
The one next after the fourth; occupying the fifth place.
Fills; makes full; satisfies or occupies completely.
Defiled, foul, or morally base.
Findest; thou findest; you find or discover.
It may also mean you obtain or come to know by experience.
Finds; discovers; comes upon.
Often also obtains or proves by experience.
Flatters; speaks smooth words to please or deceive.
Flees; runs away; escapes.
The bodily nature of man or beast.
Often man's frail, natural, and corrupt condition as opposed to the Spirit.
Belonging to the flesh; governed by bodily or natural desires.
Flows; runs in a stream; issues forth freely.
Follows; comes after; goes in the way of another.
Often also means imitates, obeys, or pursues.
Folly, want of wisdom, or that which seems foolish to natural judgment.
Since; because; inasmuch as.
To refrain, hold back, or cease from a thing.
Also to bear patiently with another.
Patient restraint; holding back what one has power to do.
A wooded region; a tract thick with trees.
To remit, pardon, or release from debt or offense.
Forgives; remits the offense; releases from the debt or guilt of a wrong.
Sexual uncleanness; unlawful sexual commerce.
Forsakes; leaves, abandons, or gives up.
Forward or outward from a place; out into view or action.
Frequently used to mark going out, being brought out, or proceeding onward.
Eager, ready, or zealous.
At times over-bold or presumptuous, depending on context.
That which is laid as the base of a building.
Figuratively, the ground, beginning, or settled basis upon which something stands.
The one next after the thirteenth; occupying the fourteenth place.
The one next after the third; occupying the fourth place.
To form, shape, or fashion.
Also the formed structure or inward constitution of a thing.
Perverse; willfully contrary; hard to govern or persuade.
Perverseness; stubborn contrary temper.
Bearing fruit; productive; yielding increase.
Also rich in good works or spiritual increase.
Fullness; the state of being full or complete.
Often abundance brought to completion or the whole measure appointed.
To speak against, contradict, or oppose by words.
A piece of clothing; an article of raiment worn upon the body.
A granary or storehouse for grain.
Collected together; assembled; brought into one place.
In some contexts also taken in, harvested, or brought to one's people in death.
Gathers; collects into one; brings together.
It may also mean harvests, takes in, or draws by degrees.
Gavest; thou gavest; you gave or bestowed.
Showy; fine in outward appearance; splendidly dressed.
The people of one age or descent; an age or family line springing from common stock.
Sometimes the race or class of men marked by a certain character.
Gets; obtains; gains; begets, according to context.
Spirit; breath of life.
In the phrase 'gave up the ghost,' to yield up life.
To bind about, equip, or prepare by fastening on what is needed.
Givest; thou givest; you give, bestow, or grant.
Gives; bestows; grants.
Joy, cheerfulness, and inward delight.
Shining brightly; sparkling.
To honor, magnify, and make glorious in esteem or display.
Reverent piety; a life shaped by the fear of God and likeness to His holiness.
Goest; thou goest; you go, depart, or proceed.
Goes; proceeds; departs.
Often used of a person's course, movement, or the advancing of a matter.
Fair, handsome, stately, or of pleasing appearance.
Moral excellence, kindness, and beneficent quality.
The good tidings, especially the glad message of Christ and salvation in Him.
A ruler, overseer, or one set over others in authority.
Favor freely shown, especially the unmerited favor of God.
Also gracious influence and beauty of manner, according to context.
Full of grace, favor, kindness, and readiness to show mercy.
Weighty, serious, or dignified.
Not only the burial place.
Seriousness, dignity, and weightiness of behavior.
Greatest; chiefest in size, rank, weight, or importance among those compared.
Great extent, dignity, power, or excellence.
Heavy, painful, burdensome, or severe.
Grows; increases; develops; waxes greater.
To murmur, begrudge, or feel ill will at another's good.
Subtle deceit, craft, or treacherous cunning.
A coat or breastpiece of armor, commonly of mail.
A dwelling place, abode, or settled place of residence.
To make holy, set apart, or treat as sacred.
To limp; to walk lamely.
In some places, to hesitate or waver between two courses.
Perhaps; by chance; it may be.
To make hard, stubborn, or insensitive.
Harshness, severity, or stubborn insensibility.
Often the hardening of heart against truth.
The reaping and gathering of ripe grain or other produce.
By figure, a season of ingathering, reward, or judgment.
An older second-person singular form meaning 'have.'
Makes haste; moves or acts quickly.
Hatest; thou hatest; you hate or regard with settled aversion.
Hates; regards with aversion or settled opposition.
An older third-person singular form meaning 'has.'
Proud loftiness; arrogance of spirit.
Heardest; thou heardest; you heard or gave ear to.
Hearest; thou hearest; you hear.
Frequently implies that you listen with attention or heed what is spoken.
Hears; receives by the ear; listens to.
Frequently implies heeding, attending, or obeying what is heard.
To listen attentively; to give ear with obedience or regard.
The nations, especially those outside the covenant people and ignorant of the true God.
Sorrow, grief, or burden of spirit.
Sometimes literal weight, but often inward sadness.
Careful attention; watchful regard.
As a verb, to give careful attention to.
One who receives an inheritance or stands to receive it by right.
From this place; away; from this cause or time.
From this time forward; hereafter.
An inheritance, possession, or allotted portion received as one's own.
To conceal, cover, or keep from sight.
Also to withdraw oneself from view or notice.
Hidest; thou hidest; you hide or conceal.
Hides; conceals; keeps from view or notice.
Highest; most high, lofty, or exalted among those compared.
To obstruct, delay, or keep back from progress.
To this place; here.
Up to this time; until now; thus far.
Holdest; thou holdest; you hold, keep fast, consider, or maintain.
The exact force depends on context.
Holds; keeps, grasps, contains, or maintains.
The exact force depends on context.
The state of being holy, set apart, and pure before God.
Set apart unto God; pure, sacred, and morally clean.
Honorable, decent, or worthy of respect.
Often carrying the sense of morally seemly as well as truthful.
Worthy of honor; held in esteem; dignified.
Honours; gives esteem, respect, or due value to.
Confident expectation of good; a looking forward with trust.
Hopes; expects with desire and confidence.
All belonging to one house or family, including servants and dependents.
The master or head of a household.
Nevertheless; yet; notwithstanding.
Lowly in mind; not proud or self-exalting.
As a verb, to bring low or cause to be lowly.
Lowliness of mind; a modest and submissive temper.
Dissimulation in religion or morals; a false outward show concealing the inward reality.
Lack of knowledge; unawareness; uninstructed condition.
Lacking knowledge; uninstructed in the matter at hand.
Not necessarily stupid, but uninformed.
A likeness, representation, or formed resemblance.
At times an idol or graven representation.
Freedom from death; endless life and incorruption.
To reckon, attribute, or set to one's account.
In so far as; seeing that; because.
Growth, multiplication, or addition.
As a verb, to make or become greater.
In truth; truly; really.
Strong displeasure or anger stirred by what is wrong.
Weakness, frailty, or feebleness, whether bodily or moral.
To dwell in, occupy, or live within.
That which is received as a possession, lot, or heritage.
Often an allotted possession from God or by family right.
Moral crookedness, unrighteousness, or guilt.
Often wickedness considered in its perverse and blameworthy character.
Urgent, earnest, or pressing in action or request.
Sometimes ready at hand or immediately present, but often carrying the sense of persistence and earnestness.
Earnestly; urgently; with pressing persistence.
Sometimes also without delay, but not limited to that.
Teaching, training, and discipline imparted for guidance.
Pleading on behalf of another; mediation by prayer or request.
The declaring or unfolding of the meaning of a thing.
Often the explanation of a dream, vision, saying, or tongue.
Within; on the inside; turned toward the inner part.
Also pertaining to the inner man, hidden thought, or secret affection.
Traveling; going on a journey or course from place to place.
Full of joy; glad and rejoicing.
To discern, decide, or pronounce sentence.
Also to govern or rule in some contexts.
Those who judge; rulers or officers appointed to decide causes.
Also the book title or governing deliverers of Israel, according to context.
Judgest; thou judgest; you judge, decide, or pronounce upon.
Sentence, decision, or act of judging.
Also justice, right order, wise discernment, or the punishment awarded by authority, according to context.
Righteous, upright, and conformable to what is right.
Also fair or due in judgment and dealing.
Righteous judgment; that which is right and due in law or moral order.
The act of being accounted righteous; acquittal in judgment.
To account or declare righteous.
Sometimes to vindicate or show to be right.
To guard, preserve, observe, or retain.
Keepest; thou keepest; you keep, guard, or observe.
Often used of observing commandments or preserving something with care.
Keeps; guards; observes; preserves.
Often means to hold fast or carefully observe a command, charge, or way.
Kills; puts to death.
In some figurative uses it means brings death, condemnation, or lifelessness.
Goodness of disposition shown in gentle and helpful dealing.
Sometimes covenant favor or loving-kindness.
Relatives, family stock, or those of the same kin.
The realm, rule, or reign of a king.
Often the dominion or royal rule of God.
Knewest; thou knewest; you knew or had knowledge of.
Knobs or rounded ornamental projections.
Knowest; thou knowest; you know, perceive, or acknowledge.
Knows; has knowledge of; perceives or acknowledges.
Understanding, acquaintance, or true apprehension of a matter.
To mourn aloud or express sorrow deeply.
Mourning expressed in cries, words, or song.
Languishes; grows faint, weak, or drooping.
Allowed by right, law, or just rule.
Lays; places; sets down; imposes.
It may also mean to direct, apply, or prepare.
Leadest; thou leadest; you lead or guide.
Leads; guides; conducts in a way.
To rest or depend upon.
Also thin or lacking fat in the literal sense.
Falsehood; lies; vanity in speech.
Leaves; departs from; lets remain; abandons.
The measure from end to end; long extent.
Smaller in amount, degree, or number; not so much.
For fear that; so that not; to avoid the danger that.
To hinder; to restrain; to hold back.
In older use, the very opposite of the common modern sense 'to allow.'
Bountiful; generous; free in giving.
Freedom from restraint or bondage.
Often lawful freedom under right order, not lawless license.
Lies; rests; is laid down.
It may also mean remains in a certain state, is situated, or lies concealed.
Liftest; thou liftest; you lift, raise up, or exalt.
Lifts; raises up; exalts.
Of one mind; sharing the same inward disposition.
Resemblance, similarity, or form answering to another thing.
Sometimes an image or representation.
In like manner; in the same way.
Living; full of life and active power.
Often simply 'living' in older usage.
Lives; remains alive; continues in life.
In some places, lives in a certain manner or under a certain rule.
Alive; having life.
Sometimes livelihood or means of support, according to context.
The lower part of the body about the hips and reins.
Often used figuratively for strength, readiness, or posterity.
Patient endurance under provocation; slowness to anger.
Lookest; thou lookest; you look or behold.
At times it also means you regard or take notice of.
Looks; beholds; directs the eyes toward.
At times it also means to regard, attend to, or expect.
Lovest; thou lovest; you love, delight in, or bear affection toward.
Loves; bears affection toward; delights in.
In Scripture often includes settled goodwill, covenant kindness, or holy delight rather than mere feeling.
Steadfast and tender covenant love expressed in mercy and favor.
Lowest; least high, least exalted, or most humble among those compared.
Humility; a lowly mind free from proud self-exaltation.
Strong desire or craving.
Often a corrupt and unlawful desire, though the bare sense is broader.
Lusts; strongly desires, often in a corrupt or unlawful way.
Madest; thou madest; you made, formed, or caused.
To make great in esteem, praise, or apparent importance.
Greatness, royal dignity, and exalted splendor.
Makest; thou makest; you make, form, or cause.
Makes; forms; causes; brings about.
Ill will, spite, or settled desire to injure.
Plainly shown; openly revealed; made evident.
Way, kind, fashion, or custom of doing or being.
To observe closely, note, or take heed of.
Also a sign, token, or target.
The womb.
Often used in the phrase 'openeth the matrix' for the firstborn issuing from the womb.
May; art permitted or enabled to.
Often expresses permission, possibility, or fitness rather than certainty.
Low in rank or outward state; humble or common.
Also to intend or signify, according to context.
Means; intends; signifies.
Food in general; nourishment.
Only sometimes flesh specifically.
One who stands between parties to reconcile or transact on behalf of both.
Gentle, lowly, and under restraint rather than self-asserting.
Strength governed, not weakness without courage.
Fit, suitable, or proper.
Not chiefly an encounter, though that sense exists in other forms.
Melts; dissolves, softens, or loses firmness.
It may also speak of the heart failing with fear or sorrow.
Goods bought and sold; traffic in wares.
Disposed to show mercy; compassionate toward misery or guilt.
Compassion shown to the miserable or guilty; pity joined with help or pardon.
When spoken of God, his gracious readiness to spare, forgive, and do good to the undeserving.
The middle, center, or inner part among surrounding things.
Mightest; thou mightest; you might, could, or would be able to.
Remembering; paying heed; taking thought for.
To serve, attend, or supply what is needed.
Whether in practical service or sacred office depends on context.
Gladness, merriment, or festive joy.
Gentleness, restraint, and a yielding spirit that avoids excess.
A stated division of time, commonly reckoned by the moon.
Besides; further; in addition to this.
To put to death; to subdue and crush the power of sinful practices.
To grieve, lament, or sorrow deeply.
Mourns; grieves; laments.
The bodily organ of speech and eating.
By extension, speech itself, command, opening, edge, or entrance, according to context.
Moves; changes place; stirs or influences.
A truth once hidden, now made known by divine revelation.
Not merely something puzzling, but something disclosed by God.
The state of being uncovered or unclothed.
Often also shameful exposure, especially where modesty, kinship, or ceremonial defilement is involved.
A people joined in common descent, land, or civil order.
Often used of the Gentile peoples as distinguished from Israel.
Worthless, evil, or of no account.
Sometimes simply nothing.
No; and more than that; a strong denial or correction.
Needs; requires; stands in need of.
Sneezings.
A bird's dwelling or resting place.
By figure, a settled and often lofty place of security.
At no time; not ever.
Notwithstanding that; for all that; yet despite what has just been said.
Near; close at hand; drawing near.
The one next after the eighth; occupying the ninth place.
The northern quarter or region.
A solemn appeal to God in confirmation of a word, promise, or testimony.
Submission to rightful authority; heedful compliance with command.
An offering presented unto God, especially one solemnly brought before him.
A cause of stumbling, falling, or displeasure.
Sometimes an actual trespass or wrongdoing.
To cause to stumble, sin, or take hurt.
Also to commit an offense against another.
Offers; presents; brings forward.
Often used of presenting sacrifice or setting something before God.
That which is brought and presented before God or another.
Often a sacrificial gift.
That which is scoured off as refuse.
Hence, something treated as vile, rejected, or despised.
Often; frequently.
Opens; makes open; reveals or begins access.
It may also mean to unfold understanding or to loose what was shut.
To crush, burden, or treat harshly by misuse of power.
Divine utterances or inspired sayings entrusted from God.
An appointed order, statute, or prescribed observance.
Ornamental settings or sockets, especially for precious stones.
To conquer, prevail over, or gain the victory against.
Overcomes; prevails against; gains victory over.
One who shares in a thing; a participant or partner.
In part; to some extent; not wholly.
Passest; thou passest; you pass, go by, move onward, or surpass.
Passes; goes by; moves onward.
It may also mean surpasses, comes to an end, or is transferred from one state to another.
Steadfast endurance under trial; a calm continuing under burden without giving way.
Enduring calmly; able to bear delay, trial, or injury without murmuring.
Specially one's own; set apart as a treasured possession.
Not merely odd or strange.
Perhaps; it may be; possibly.
Complete, whole, or mature.
Often full-grown or fully furnished rather than absolutely sinless.
Completeness, maturity, or full accomplishment.
To be ruined, lost, or destroyed.
Distressed with uncertainty; puzzled and troubled.
To pursue with hostility; to harass or oppress for hatred's sake.
Convinced, assured, or won over in judgment.
A settled conviction or belief.
Also the act or power of winning over another's judgment.
Pertains; belongs properly to; relates to.
A grievous contagious disease; plague.
Often one sent as a severe public judgment.
A formal request, earnest asking, or supplication.
Urinates.
Used in the older literal expression "him that pisseth against the wall."
Full of pity; compassionate and tenderhearted.
Not chiefly miserable in the modern sense.
Tender compassion toward one in misery or weakness.
Also the act of sparing or having mercy.
Clear, open, and easy to understand.
Also level or straightforward.
Openness, simplicity, and straightforward clearness.
Delightful, agreeable, or giving satisfaction.
Pleases; gives satisfaction to; is agreeable to.
Abundant; full; richly supplied.
To defile, profane, or make unclean.
Lacking worldly goods; needy; lowly in outward estate.
At times humble and dependent in spirit.
A part allotted, assigned, or belonging to one as a share.
To hold as one's own; to occupy; to have mastery over.
That which one owns or holds as property; an occupied holding.
Pours; causes to flow out; sheds forth abundantly.
Mighty in effect, force, or ability.
Commendation, thanksgiving, and spoken honor given for worth or goodness.
As a verb, to extol or magnify.
To ask earnestly; to entreat; to make supplication.
Most often in Scripture, to call upon God with petition, confession, thanksgiving, or praise.
Prayest; thou prayest; you pray, ask earnestly, or make supplication.
To proclaim publicly, especially as a herald or preacher.
Costly, highly valued, and greatly esteemed.
The fact of being present; nearness before a person.
Often the face, favor, or immediate appearing of a king or of God.
Immediately; before long; soon.
To keep safe, guard from harm, or maintain from ruin.
Preserves; keeps safe; guards from harm or decay.
To prove stronger, gain the victory, or have effect.
To go before; to come before; to anticipate in time.
Not chiefly to stop from happening, as in modern usage.
The office, order, or collective body of priests.
Those appointed to minister in sacred service and offer sacrifices.
Ruling powers or chief authorities.
Often spiritual ranks or governing powers.
Proceeds; goes forth; comes out from; continues onward.
To announce publicly; to publish aloud.
Common rather than holy; irreverent toward sacred things.
Gain, advantage, or beneficial increase.
Profits; is of gain, use, or advantage.
Progress, gain, or advancement.
A pledged word; an assured declaration of what shall be given or done.
Prophesies; speaks forth under divine impulse or declares what God reveals.
That which turns away wrath and restores favor by a sacrificial satisfaction.
To succeed, advance, or go forward with good issue.
A state of flourishing, welfare, and successful condition.
To test, try, and find out by trial.
Also to demonstrate or show to be so.
To stir up, incite, or move strongly.
Often to anger, but sometimes to good works.
Wise foresight and practical judgment in conduct.
Wise in practical judgment; discreet and circumspect.
To proclaim openly; to make known abroad.
Clean, unmixed, and free from defilement.
Purity, cleanness, and freedom from defilement.
To cleanse thoroughly by removing what defiles.
To cleanse, refine, or make pure.
A set intention, design, or settled resolve.
Pursues; follows after eagerly; chases or seeks to overtake.
Puttest; thou puttest; you put, place, set, or lay upon.
Puts; places; sets.
Often also lays upon, appoints, or causes to be in a certain state.
Living; alive.
Sometimes lively, active, or piercing, according to context.
To make alive; to give life or renewed vigor.
Also to revive spiritually.
Swiftly, soon, or without delay.
To acquit or free from obligation.
In 'quit you like men,' to conduct yourselves bravely or manfully.
Clothing; garments; apparel.
Water descending from the clouds.
As a verb, to pour down or cause to fall abundantly.
Raises; lifts up; causes to arise or stand.
It may also mean stirs up, appoints, or brings forth.
Reaches; extends to; attains or comes as far as.
Resistance against rightful rule; willful rising against authority.
To reprove sharply; to check by word of authority.
At times to restrain or suppress by command.
To take, accept, or welcome what is given or brought.
Repayment, return, or reward rendered for good or evil.
To restore to favor, peace, or agreement after estrangement.
To buy back, ransom, or deliver by payment or costly means.
Deliverance secured by ransom; release obtained at a price.
A shelter, defense, or place of safety.
Refuses; declines; will not accept or obey.
To look upon with consideration; to heed or respect.
Also esteem or favorable notice.
Regards; looks upon; esteems or takes notice of.
New birth; renewal into new life.
At times also a great renewing or restoration.
Reigns; rules as king or sovereign; exercises prevailing dominion.
The inward parts, especially the kidneys, figuratively the seat of feeling or hidden affections.
To be glad, exult, or take joy.
Joyful exultation; the act or state of rejoicing.
Remains; continues; is left over after other things are removed or finished.
To call to mind, keep in mind, or act with mindful regard toward.
Called to mind; kept in remembrance; regarded with mindful attention.
Memory; mindful recollection; a keeping in mind.
Also a memorial or token that calls something to mind.
Release, cancellation, or forgiveness, especially of sins.
To give back, pay, or return what is due.
To make fresh again; to restore or strengthen anew.
To turn in mind and purpose; to feel sorrow for sin and turn from it.
In some contexts it may speak more generally of relenting or changing course.
A change of mind and heart leading to turning from sin unto God.
Repentest; thou repentest; you repent, turn in mind, or feel sorrow for wrong and turn from it.
Reproaches; brings shame upon; taunts or upbraids.
Correction, rebuke, or blame given for fault.
A petition, desire, or thing asked for.
Also the act of making such a petition.
To demand, ask for, or call for as due.
Relief from labor or disturbance; settled repose.
Also the remainder or what is left over, according to context.
Rising again from the dead; restoration to life.
Returns; comes back; turns again.
To rail at, speak abusively against, or heap insulting words upon.
Recompense, return, or what is given in response to service or conduct.
Abundant wealth or valuable possession.
Also abundance of spiritual treasure, according to context.
Rides; is carried along upon a beast or vehicle.
Just, upright, and in the right according to God's standard.
Justice, uprightness, and conformity to what is right before God.
Wasteful, disorderly excess; loose and lavish living.
Rises; gets up; stands up; springs forth.
It may also mean arises in power, appears, or comes into action.
Place; space; opportunity.
Not necessarily an enclosed chamber.
Untrained, unskilled, or plain rather than polished.
Not necessarily insolent in the modern sense.
Rules; governs; exercises authority over.
Runs; moves swiftly; hastens.
It may also be said of water or of the course of a thing.
A day of sacred rest set apart unto God.
By use it may speak of the weekly holy day or of other solemn rest-days appointed under the law.
A coarse cloth, often of hair, worn in mourning, humiliation, or penitence.
Says; speaks; declares.
A greeting; words of welcome or respectful address.
Deliverance, preservation, and rescue, especially from sin and judgment.
Made holy; set apart unto sacred use or cleansed for God.
Sanctifies; sets apart as holy; consecrates or makes holy.
To set apart as holy; to consecrate.
Also to cleanse and make holy in life and use.
A holy place set apart for God's worship or presence.
To fill, content, or supply fully.
Saves; delivers, preserves, or rescues from danger or ruin.
Taste, smell, or perceived quality.
Figuratively, inward relish, bent, or judgment.
Sawest; thou sawest; you saw or beheld.
Sayest; thou sayest; you say or speak.
Hardly; barely; with difficulty.
Also rare or not plentiful in some contexts.
Scatters; disperses; causes to be spread abroad.
To despise with mockery or proud contempt.
Also the expression of such contempt.
A mocker; one who treats truth or correction with proud contempt.
A small bag, wallet, or satchel for carrying provisions.
To examine carefully, seek out, or inquire diligently.
Seekest; thou seekest; you seek, look for, or pursue after.
Seeks; looks for; pursues after.
Often implies earnest desire, inquiry, or effort to obtain.
Seems; appears; is judged or thought to be so.
Becoming, decent, fitting, or proper.
Seest; thou seest; you see or perceive.
Often carries the further sense of understanding or taking notice.
Sees; beholds; perceives.
Often includes understanding, taking notice, or discerning.
Sendest; thou sendest; you send or dispatch.
Sends; dispatches; causes to go forth.
One who serves another under authority.
The word may refer to a household servant, official minister, or bond-servant of God.
Serves; ministers to; is in bondage or obedience to.
Settest; thou settest; you set, place, appoint, or fix.
Sets; places; appoints; establishes.
To establish, fix firmly, or bring into a stable state.
The one next after the sixth; occupying the seventh place.
Separate; distinct; set apart from one another.
Sharpness, strictness, or stern dealing.
Shade or dark outline caused by intercepted light.
Figuratively, a dim image, shelter, or foreshadowing of something greater.
Shakes; causes to tremble, move, or be disturbed.
Will; is to; must; is bound to, according to context.
Often expresses certainty, duty, or future determination.
A meat market; a place where butchered flesh is sold.
Dishonor, reproach, or the painful sense of disgrace.
Modesty joined with a wholesome sense of shame and reserve.
Not fear of faces, but modest self-restraint.
To show; to make plain or visible.
Shewest; thou shewest; you show, make known, or set before the sight.
Shows; makes known; sets before the sight or understanding.
An older spelling of 'showeth.'
Shines; gives light; appears bright or glorious.
Soon; in a little time; quickly.
Shouldest; thou shouldest; you ought to, would be expected to, or would in fitting course.
Shows; makes known; displays; causes to be seen.
Shuts; closes; keeps from entrance or exit.
Stillness or absence of speech.
Also the act of refraining from speech or the bringing of speech to an end.
Weak, helpless, or pitiable.
Sometimes simple or easily deceived, not merely playful foolishness.
Inexperienced; easily led; lacking sound judgment.
Not merely plain or uncomplicated.
Singleness, sincerity, or freedom from mixture and guile.
Not merely lack of complexity.
Purity, singleness, and freedom from hypocrisy or mixture.
Sins; commits sin; acts contrary to God's law.
Since; seeing that.
Sittest; thou sittest; you sit or are seated.
It may also mean you dwell or remain in a settled place.
Sits; is seated; remains in a sitting place.
Often also means dwells, abides in rule, or occupies a settled position.
The one next after the fifth; occupying the sixth place.
To kill, especially by violence.
Sleeps; rests in sleep.
At times used more gently of death.
Smites; strikes, wounds, or afflicts.
A trap or entangling device.
Figuratively, anything that draws one into danger or sin.
Self-restrained; sound in mind; temperate and serious.
To boil or seethe.
Sojourns; dwells as a stranger for a time; resides temporarily.
Serious, grave, and set apart for weighty use or worship.
A certain portion or number not fully specified.
Sometimes certain persons or things distinguished from others.
Greatly; grievously; painfully severe.
Also painful or afflicted in the literal sense.
Grief, sadness, or inward pain.
The living soul or self; the life, person, or inward being of a man.
According to context it may refer to the whole person, the seat of desire and feeling, or life itself.
The southern quarter or region.
Sows; scatters seed; plants for increase.
Figuratively, it may mean to spread or produce something that will later bear fruit.
An older past-tense form meaning 'spoke.'
Spakest; thou spakest; you spoke or uttered words.
To utter words, say, or make known by speech.
An older second-person singular form meaning 'speakest' or 'you speak.'
Speaks; utters words; declares.
Breath, spirit, or the immaterial principle of life and inward disposition.
It may refer to the human spirit, an angelic spirit, an evil spirit, or the Spirit of God according to context.
Spreads; stretches out; extends or disperses abroad.
To establish; to make firm, settled, or stable.
Stablishes; makes stable, firm, or fixed.
An older form of establish with much the same force.
Standest; thou standest; you stand or remain fixed.
Often means you remain present, firm, or established before another.
Stands; remains upright or fixed.
Often means continues firm, remains established, or is present ready before another.
One entrusted with the management of another's goods or household.
Narrow, close, or difficult to pass through.
Also pressed or distressed by difficulty.
Straitens; narrows, presses, or confines.
Often means to distress, hem in, or make difficult.
Straitest; most narrow, close, or difficult among those compared.
It may also mean most pressed or most confined.
Strictly; closely; with sharp charge or narrow constraint.
Narrowness, confinement, or distress arising from being pressed in.
Power, force, or ability whether of body, mind, heart, or estate.
Often firmness and capacity to endure or prevail.
Strengthens; makes strong; confirms or supports.
Stretches; extends or spreads forth.
To contend, labor earnestly, or struggle toward a thing.
To be diligent; to strive earnestly after a thing.
Not only to read books, but to give labor and care.
To yield, subject oneself, or place under authority.
Possession, wealth, or material goods.
Sometimes the real underlying reality or essence.
Subtle, crafty, or keenly discerning.
The moral tone depends on context.
To allow; to permit.
Also to endure pain or wrong, according to context.
Several; diverse; of various sorts.
An overflowing excess; abundance beyond what is fitting.
In moral use, rank overgrowth of wickedness.
Earnest pleading; humble entreaty in prayer.
To think, assume, or reckon as likely.
Certain, firm, secure, or reliable.
Certainly; assuredly; without doubt.
Security or pledge given for another.
A person who becomes bound on behalf of another.
Wrapping closely with bands of cloth, especially an infant.
Swarest; thou swarest; you swore, especially by oath.
To bind oneself with an oath or solemn declaration.
Swears; takes an oath or speaks with solemn appeal.
Takest; thou takest; you take, receive, or lay hold on.
Takes; receives; lays hold on.
It may also mean removes, carries away, or accepts according to context.
To remain, stay, or wait.
Teachest; thou teachest; you teach or instruct.
Teaches; instructs; imparts knowledge or doctrine.
Tears; rends or pulls apart violently.
The hard parts in the mouth used for biting and chewing.
Often used figuratively of devouring power, rage, or fierce injury.
Tells; relates, declares, or counts.
To mix, soften, or bring into a fitting condition.
Also inward disposition or frame of spirit in some uses.
Self-control; mastery over appetites and passions.
A violent storm, especially with wind and disturbance.
To try, test, or prove.
Often to entice toward evil, according to context.
Soft, delicate, or gentle.
Often compassionate and easily touched in feeling.
The one next after the ninth; occupying the tenth place.
Great dread, fear, or cause of fearful trembling.
Witness borne; solemn declaration; evidence given.
Also God's revealed witness or covenantal charge.
The giving of thanks; grateful acknowledgment offered to God.
From that place; from that source; from there.
In that place; at that point; in that matter, according to context.
By that means; through that.
For that reason; on that account; consequently.
In that; within that matter or place.
Of that; belonging to that; concerning that.
On that; upon that thing or place.
To that; unto that; in addition to that.
With that; together with it; by means of that.
Thinkest; thou thinkest; you think, suppose, or consider.
The one next after the twenty-ninth; occupying the thirtieth place.
To that place; there.
Toward that place; in that direction.
Touches; comes into contact with.
It may also mean reaches, affects, or concerns.
In the direction of; with respect to; near unto.
Went beyond the commanded boundary; broke the law or charge given.
Transgressest; thou transgressest; you go beyond the command and so offend.
A going beyond the boundary of law; offense against command.
Painful labor, especially labor in childbirth.
By extension, severe toil or inward anguish.
Travailest; thou travailest; you labor painfully, especially as in sore toil or birth-pain.
Treads; steps upon; walks over; tramples.
To shake with fear, weakness, or strong feeling.
Pressure, affliction, and distress under trial.
Troubles; disturbs, afflicts, or throws into agitation.
To think, suppose, or believe.
To rely upon with confidence; to place dependence in.
Trustest; thou trustest; you trust, rely, or place confidence in.
That which is true, sure, and according to reality.
Also faithfulness, steadfastness, and sincerity, especially when spoken of God or of covenant dealing.
Turnest; thou turnest; you turn, return, or change course.
Turns; changes direction; returns.
Often used of turning the heart, changing course, or converting from one state or way to another.
Two; a pair considered as two persons or things.
The one next after the nineteenth; occupying the twentieth place.
Without being aware; unexpectedly; before one perceives it.
Not circumcised; outside the covenant sign.
Often used figuratively for what is unrenewed or unpurified.
Defiled, impure, or ceremonially unfit.
At times morally impure as well.
Impurity, whether ceremonial or moral.

Understands; perceives the meaning of; discerns rightly.
Discernment, intelligence, and right apprehension of a matter.
Without hypocrisy; sincere; genuine.
Without godliness; irreverent and wicked toward God.
Injustice, wrong, or want of conformity to what is right before God.
To; toward; until; in relation to, according to context.
Straight, just, and morally sound.
With straightness, integrity, and moral rightness.
Toward a higher place; above.
Custom, practice, or habitual way of acting.
Also employment or application of a thing.
Uses; employs; is accustomed to.
Interest exacted on a loan, especially when viewed as oppressive or unlawful.
Utters; speaks forth; gives out.
Farthest, highest, or most extreme limit.
Empty, fruitless, or without true profit.
Also proud or lifted up, according to context.
To no good purpose; fruitlessly; emptily.
Emptiness, frailty, and what proves passing or profitless.
Sometimes pride or self-regard, according to context.
Avenging justice; the inflicting of due punishment for wrong.
Truly; assuredly; of a truth.
Troubling, irritation, or grievous disquiet.
Low, base, or morally contemptible.
Sometimes humble or lowly in outward estate, depending on context.
Force used wrongfully; oppression, cruelty, or violent injury.
Moral excellence or goodness.
In some contexts, effective power or efficacy, as when virtue goes out from a person.
A sight, appearance, or supernatural revelation seen.
Often a divinely granted scene or message perceived by prophetic sight.
A coming to inspect, help, or judge.
May be gracious visitation or punitive visitation depending on context.
Calling; the state or duty to which one is called.
Waits; stays in expectation; tarries with hope or readiness.
Walkest; thou walkest; you walk.
Very often used of the way you live and conduct yourself.
Walks; goes on foot.
Very often means lives, conducts oneself, or proceeds in a course of life.
To go astray, roam, or move without a settled course.
Wants; lacks; is without what is needed.
Unrestrained; luxuriant; self-indulgent.
Often carrying the sense of licentious or undisciplined behavior.
An older second-person singular form meaning 'wert' or 'you were.'
To stay awake, keep guard, or remain spiritually alert.
Also a division of the night under a guard system.
Alert, vigilant, and careful to observe danger or duty.
To grow, become, or increase.
Lack of strength, whether bodily, mental, or spiritual.
Wentest; thou wentest; you went, departed, or proceeded.
The western quarter or region.
Toward the west.
Whatever at all; anything whatever without exception.
From what place; from which source.
Whenever; at whatever time.
In what place; in which place; at the place in view.
Sometimes used more broadly of the situation or matter in question.
While on the other hand; considering that; where.
By which; through which means.
For which reason; why; on what account.
In which; in what thing or matter.
Of which; concerning which.
On which; upon which thing or place.
In whatever place; wherever.
To which; unto which thing or purpose.
With which; by means of which.
During the time that; as long as; for a short time.
To what place; to which place.
To whatever place; wherever one may go.
Entirely; fully; altogether.
Whoever; any person whatsoever without naming which one in particular.
Evil in character and conduct; guilty of moral wrong.
In a wicked manner; evilly; with moral wrong or impiety.
Evil conduct, moral wrong, or active ungodliness.
A desert place, waste, or uncultivated region fit for little settled habitation.
Often a solitary tract lying apart from cities and fields.
Crafty methods, subtle schemes, or deceitful devices.
Right judgment joined with understanding and skill in godly living.
Endued with sound judgment, understanding, and skill in right conduct.
Sometimes prudent in earthly affairs, but often morally and spiritually discerning.
Knew.
An older past-tense form related to 'wit' in the sense of knowing.
With the rest; besides; together with; at the same time.
Dried up, shrunk, or wasted away for lack of life or moisture.
Withers; dries up; wastes away for lack of life or moisture.
One who bears testimony, or the testimony itself that is borne.
Also a token, evidence, or public sign that proves or confirms a matter.
Works; acts; performs or produces an effect.
Often means operates inwardly or brings something to pass.
Honor, reverence, and adoration offered to one deemed worthy.
In Scripture most often the reverent service and homage due unto God.
Fit, suitable, deserving, or of corresponding value.
Know; knoweth; knows.
Wouldest; thou wouldest; you would, wish, or were inclined to.
Often expresses willingness, desire, or what one would do in a supposed case.
Strong anger or indignation.
Often righteous judgment when spoken of God.
Angry; filled with wrath.
Least in age; the most young among those compared.
The early part of life; young age; the state of being young.
Fervent eagerness and ardor of spirit for a cause or person.
